steps.title: Test for a specific response
steps.content: Alvarez and colleagues concluded that S. leucostigma associates T. caesius alarm calls with danger because the birds froze or scattered after hearing those calls. Strong support should show that the response was specific to the alarm calls, not merely a reaction to researchers approaching, to any random sound, or to general disturbance in the area.

steps.title: Use the control result
steps.content: Choice A suggests a possible confound because the birds sometimes reacted before sounds were played. Choice C concerns other bird species, not whether S. leucostigma recognizes these calls. Choice D shows consistent responses to different callers, but not specificity compared with other sounds. No reaction to random control sounds would show that the defensive response was tied to T. caesius alarm calls in particular. The answer is B.
